signals.idea: Add a final video available marker to shot groups in the video editor timeline. When a shot group has a final video (from shot_final_videos table), show a video icon badge on the right side of the shot group overlay (similar to stale variant amber badge). Clicking opens a panel with: 1) Switch to final video - removes all image clips in that shot group and replaces with a single final video clip spanning the same time range, 2) Dismiss reminder - hides the marker. Follow existing patterns: stale variant marker (useStaleVariants.ts, ClipAction.tsx), shot groups (useShotGroups.ts, TimelineCanvas.tsx), final video data (useShotFinalVideos.ts), asset registration (useAssetManagement.ts). Key replacement logic: register final video as asset, calculate time span of shot group clips, remove those clips, insert single final video clip, persist changes.

unresolved_flags.concern: Shot-group replacement targeting: Recurring debt: the plan keys the switch action by `shotId`, but `useShotGroups()` can produce multiple distinct groups for the same shot, so the replacement target is ambiguous and can hit the wrong row or clip set.

Asset registration contract: the proposed `registerGenerationAsset()` call uses an unsupported `mimeType` field, so final videos may not be registered as video assets unless the plan passes `variantType: 'video'` or `metadata.content_type`.
Verification coverage: the planned checks do not exercise the new shot-group badge/menu wiring or the atomic replace mutation, so the main risk surface remains untested.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the requested interaction against the existing timeline UI in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:343-364,774-803` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:107-201`. The repo’s established pattern for these affordances is a portal context menu, but the user request explicitly says clicking the badge should open a panel; the plan changes that to a menu/mini-menu, which is a product-level divergence that needs an explicit acceptance rather than being treated as equivalent.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked how shot groups are defined in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:29-33,73-75` and in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts:101-110`. The current repo only materializes shot groups when there are at least two matching clips, so the plan’s assumption that single-clip shots will not show the final-video badge is consistent with current code, but it still leaves one of the plan’s own open questions unresolved and narrows the feature unless that scope choice is confirmed.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the proposed asset-registration call against the actual drag-drop contract in `src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts:48-55` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:66-85`. The plan says to call `registerGenerationAsset({ generationId: finalVideo.id, imageUrl: finalVideo.location, mimeType: 'video/mp4' })`, but `mimeType` is not a supported field on `GenerationDropData`, and `registerGenerationAsset()` only looks at `metadata.content_type`, `variantType`, or the URL suffix; as written, this call can misclassify the asset unless the location reliably ends with a video extension.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked `useShotGroups()` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:79-130` and the planned `TimelineCanvas` callback shape that only passes `shotId`. The repository can emit multiple distinct groups for the same shot across rows or separated runs on one row, so a switch action keyed only by `shotId` is ambiguous and can replace the wrong clip set; the callback needs the clicked group identity (`clipIds`, `rowId`, or a group key), not just the shot id.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked the existing video-drop paths in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/external-drop-utils.ts:46-55,386-412` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:198-252`. The broader repository pattern for video assets is not just “register then insert”; multi-generation video drops also patch `registry.duration` using `getDroppedGenerationDuration()`, while `useShotFinalVideos()` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37` currently fetches no duration metadata. That means final-video replacement is broader than badge wiring alone if the new clip is expected to behave like other video assets in timing UI and resize logic.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked shot-group action plumbing in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:343-364,757-803`. The canvas already has one shot-group menu state and portal renderer for group actions, so introducing a second parallel menu state for final-video actions would duplicate the same concern in the same component; extending the existing menu model with richer group payload looks more aligned with the current structure.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 12.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 9.

items.criterion: A video icon badge (sky-500 or similar distinct color) appears on shot group overlays when that shot has a final video in shot_final_videos
Clicking the badge opens a menu with 'Switch to Final Video' and 'Dismiss' options
'Switch to Final Video' removes all image clips in the shot group and inserts a single video clip spanning the same time range
The replacement video clip is properly registered in the asset registry with correct type and generationId
'Dismiss' hides the badge for that shot (client-side only)
Existing useShotGroups.test.ts tests pass without modification
The switch action is a single undo step (one applyEdit call)
Badge styling is visually distinct from the amber stale-variant badge
New hook file stays under ~80 lines; no file exceeds +50 lines of additions
useFinalVideoAvailable has a unit test covering: shot with final video detected, dismissed shot hidden, shot without final video ignored
Manual smoke test: open editor with project that has shot final videos, badge appears, switch works, dismiss works, undo restores original clips

unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:79-130` can emit multiple groups per shot, while the planned callbacks only pass `shotId` from `TimelineCanvas`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Asset registration contract: the proposed `registerGenerationAsset()` call uses an unsupported `mimeType` field, so final videos may not be registered as video assets unless the plan passes `variantType: 'video'` or `metadata.content_type`.
